title: Cosmetic Oil on Silk
timeEstimate: Pro-lane within hours; home is bulk-lift + cold blot only
stainChemistry: Cosmetic oils on silk are the rare combination where every standard home oil-removal tool damages silk. The botanical oil + silicone + essential oil mix grabs silk fast; silicone forms a slick water-repellent film. The household toolkit fails: water alone leaves permanent rings on silk, rubbing alcohol strips silk dye, acetone destroys the fiber, hot Dawn risks setting any sweat-protein contamination. The honest path: blot bulk gently, leave the rest alone, get to a silk specialist. Silk + cosmetic oil is pro-lane within hours.
whyThisWorks: A pro spotter with cold POG / petroleum ether for the silicone phase + cold NSD for residual oil + acetic acid neutralization can clear cosmetic oil from silk without damage. Home protocol is to lift bulk wet oil with a clean cloth, leave the rest alone, and get to a specialist. The silk's best outcome is reaching the cleaner with nothing else applied.
scienceNote: Mechanism (pro-facing): cosmetic oils combine triglyceride botanical oils + silicones (dimethicone, cyclomethicone) + essential oils. Silk is fibroin protein — solvent and pH-sensitive. Pro uses cold POG / petroleum ether for silicone + cold NSD + acetic acid (60-sec dwell) for pH restoration. Sodium hydrosulfite at controlled dilution for any residual chromophore from essential-oil pigments.
defaultAssumption: Face serum / body oil drip on a silk blouse / scarf / dress. Pro-lane within hours. Home protocol = bulk-lift + cold blot only. The silk's best outcome is reaching the cleaner with nothing else applied.

spottingProtocol.technique: Plastic edge or cloth lift, no rubbing
spottingProtocol.instruction: If wet bulk visible: press a clean white cloth straight down on the pool, lift straight up, repeat 2–3 times with fresh cloth sections. Bulk surface only. NEVER rub, smear, or scrape with a sharp edge — silk silk protein tears.

spottingProtocol.equipment: POG / petroleum ether (cold) on white cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Cold solvent dissolves silicone. Tight blotting from edge inward, refresh cloth each pass. Do NOT flood silk.
Cold NSD lifts dissolved oil. Press only, no agitation. Minimum moisture to avoid water rings.
Dropper for pH restoration after solvent + NSD. STRICT 60-sec ceiling. Flush cold immediately.
Cold final rinse. Press between dry towels, lay flat to air-dry, never hang wet.

steps: 1. Triage — silk + cosmetic oil is pro-lane within hours.
2. Bulk-lift wet oil with clean white cloth, no rubbing.
3. Cold POG / petroleum ether for silicone phase.
4. Cold NSD sponge press for residual oil.
5. Acetic acid 60-sec spotter for pH restoration.
6. Cold final rinse, press dry flat.
warnings: NEVER alcohol on silk — strips silk dyes
NEVER acetone on silk — destroys silk protein
NEVER hot water — sets silk + sets any contaminant
NEVER flood silk with water — permanent rings
NEVER scrape with sharp edge — tears silk protein
Pro-lane within hours — home is bulk-lift only

homeSolutions.instruction: Cosmetic oil on silk is the rare combination where every standard home oil-removal tool damages silk. Water leaves permanent rings, rubbing alcohol strips silk dye, acetone destroys the fiber, even hot Dawn sets any sweat-protein contamination. The household oil-cleanup toolkit doesn't transfer to silk. Home protocol = bulk-lift wet oil, leave the rest alone, get to a silk specialist within hours. The silk's best outcome is reaching the cleaner with nothing else applied.

homeSolutions.agent: Wet Bulk — Lift Once, Then Stop
homeSolutions.instruction: If wet oil is still pooled on the silk, press a clean white cloth STRAIGHT DOWN on the pool — lift straight up, no smearing or rubbing. Repeat with fresh cloth sections 2 to 3 times. Bulk surface only. Don't try to lift more after 2 to 3 dabs — you'll start grinding oil into the fiber.

homeSolutions.agent: If Dry — Leave It Completely Alone
homeSolutions.instruction: If the oil has soaked in and dried, do NOT try to soften it with anything (water, alcohol, soap, solvent). Silk fragility means dry oil is BETTER for the pro to handle than disturbed oil. Just take it in.

Not every dry cleaner handles silk well. Call ahead: "Do you treat cosmetic oil on silk with cold POG / petroleum ether?" If they don't know what those are, try another cleaner. When you bring it in, tell them: "Cosmetic oil [silicone-heavy / botanical / hair oil] on silk, age [hours / days]. Bulk-lift ONLY at home with clean white cloth. NO water, no alcohol, no soap, no solvent applied."

steps: 1. Silk + cosmetic oil = pro-lane within hours.
2. If wet: 2–3 light dabs straight down with white cloth, no rubbing.
3. If dry: leave alone. Do not apply anything.
4. Pro silk specialist with cosmetic oil type identified.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop after the bulk-lift dab. Any home water, soap, alcohol, or solvent on silk creates new damage worse than the oil.
warnings: NEVER alcohol on silk — strips silk dyes
NEVER acetone on silk — destroys silk protein
NEVER water on silk for oil — permanent rings
NEVER hot Dawn on silk — sets any sweat-protein contamination
NEVER scrape dried oil with sharp edge — tears silk protein
Pro-lane within hours — bulk-lift only at home

neverDo: Never alcohol on silk — strips silk dyes
Never acetone on silk — destroys silk protein
Never water flooding on silk — permanent rings
Never scrape dried oil with sharp edge
Never improvise solvent at home — pro-only
fiberSensitivities: Silk is protein fiber — damaged by alcohol, acetone, alkaline cleaners, hot water
Silicone-heavy cosmetic oils especially difficult — silicone bonds to silk protein
Water rings on silk are as visible as the oil itself
Pro-lane chemistry (cold POG, cold NSD, acetic acid) is the only safe path

materialWarnings: Pro-lane within hours — home is bulk-lift only
Standard home oil-removal toolkit damages silk
Water leaves permanent rings on silk
Alcohol strips silk dyes; acetone destroys silk protein

escalation.when: Always — silk + cosmetic oil is pro-lane within hours. Earlier escalation if the oil has soaked in (dried), if the silk is dyed and someone applied alcohol-containing product, or if water was already poured on the spot.
escalation.whatToTell: "Cosmetic oil [silicone-heavy serum / body oil / hair oil / massage oil] on silk, age [hours / days], bulk-lift ONLY at home with clean white cloth. No water, no alcohol, no soap, no solvent applied."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner specializing in silk (call ahead — confirm cold POG / petroleum ether spotting capability)

contextQuestions: What kind of cosmetic oil — face serum, body oil, hair oil, massage oil, primer?
Silicone-heavy product (smooth slick feel) or pure botanical oil?
Wet pool or already dried in?
Type of silk — charmeuse, chiffon, habotai, dupioni?
Has anyone applied anything to the spot (water, alcohol, soap)?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Silicone-heavy serums are the harder case — silicone bonds particularly well to silk silk protein. Pure botanical oils are still pro-lane on silk but slightly easier. Thinner silks (chiffon, habotai) more vulnerable than charmeuse. Anything pre-applied at home (especially alcohol) makes the pro's job harder — disclose honestly.
